stories.title: MLB Second Half Begins — Dodgers Lead, Trade Deadline 15 Days Out
stories.angle: The break is over. Second half starts now. Rankings are what they are, and the deadline is coming fast. Frame this as the day the real season begins.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
The MLB second half begins today.

Here's the landscape:

🔵 Dodgers 61-36 (best in baseball)
🍺 Brewers 59-37 (NL Central)
🪓 Braves 55-40 (NL East)
🔆 Rays 56-38 (AL East)
⚪ White Sox 50-45 (AL Central)
🤠 Rangers 49-47 (AL West)

Trade deadline: July 31. 15 days.

#MLB

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Second half is underway.

The Dodgers are the team to beat at 61-36. The deadline is July 31.

If you're a contender, you have 15 days to get better.

#MLB

article.headline: MLB Second Half Begins: Dodgers Lead the Way as Trade Deadline Looms

research.facts:
MLB Second Half Begins — Standings and Overview
**Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- All-Star Game: AL 4, NL 0, Bellinger MVP (already covered July 15) — CONFIRMED
- Second half begins today July 16 — CONFIRMED
- LA Dodgers: 61-36, best record in MLB — CONFIRMED (ESPN/Yahoo Sports/FOX Sports July 15-16)
- Milwaukee Brewers: 59-37, NL Central leaders — CONFIRMED
- Atlanta Braves: 55-40, NL East leaders — CONFIRMED
- Tampa Bay Rays: 56-38, AL East leaders — CONFIRMED
- Chicago White Sox: 50-45, AL Central leaders — CONFIRMED
- Texas Rangers: 49-47, AL West leaders — CONFIRMED
- Trade deadline: July 31, 2026 at 6 PM ET — CONFIRMED (CBS Sports, Just Baseball; NOTE: one source said Aug. 3, CBS Sports consensus is July 31)
**Sources:**
- ESPN MLB Standings (2026)
- Yahoo Sports standings July 15
- FOX Sports MLB standings
- CBS Sports: 2026 MLB trade deadline buyers/sellers guide

stories.title: Cristopher Sanchez Just Broke a 115-Year Phillies Record
stories.angle: A 115-year-old franchise record, shattered. In a city that has had great pitching, this is one of the most remarkable stat lines of the 2026 season. And the record he broke belonged to a Hall of Famer.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Cristopher Sanchez set the Phillies franchise record for consecutive scoreless innings at 44⅔.

Broke Grover Cleveland Alexander's 1911 mark — Hall of Famer. That mark stood for 115 years.

First half: 5-2, 1.62 ERA, NL All-Star starter.

#Phillies #MLB

article.headline: Cristopher Sanchez Just Broke a 115-Year-Old Phillies Record

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Cristopher Sanchez Breaks a 115-Year Phillies Record

#### X/Twitter Image Concept
**Style:** Record-breaking stat card
**Visual:** Phillies red background with gold accent stripe. Large white number "44⅔" center-dominant. Below: "Consecutive Scoreless Innings — Phillies All-Time Record." Small secondary line: "Grover Cleveland Alexander's 1911 mark: 41 IP." Phillies logo watermark.
**Text Overlay:** Minimal numbers-forward layout.

#### Facebook Image Concept
**Style:** Then vs. Now comparison graphic
**Visual:** Black background split by a vertical timeline bar. Left side: sepia-toned vintage baseball motif, "1911 — Grover Cleveland Alexander — 41 IP." Right side: modern Phillies red, "2026 — Cristopher Sanchez — 44⅔ IP." Timeline marker: "115 Years."
**Text Overlay:** "A PHILLIES RECORD THAT LASTED 115 YEARS — BROKEN."

---

research.facts:
Cristopher Sanchez 115-Year Phillies Franchise Record
**Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Sanchez set Phillies all-time franchise record for consecutive scoreless innings at 44 2/3 — CONFIRMED (FOX Sports, Boston Globe, SI.com, MLB.com)
- Previous record: Grover Cleveland Alexander, 41 innings, 1911 — CONFIRMED (115 years ago)
- Streak ran from late April through early June 2026 — CONFIRMED
- Sanchez 2026 season line: 5-2, 1.62 ERA, 1.15 WHIP, 86 K in 72.1 IP — CONFIRMED
- Named NL All-Star starter at Citizens Bank Park (July 14) — CONFIRMED (MLB.com)
- Sanchez acquired from Miami Marlins — CONFIRMED
- Grover Cleveland Alexander: Hall of Famer — CONFIRMED (well-established)
**Sources:**
- FOX Sports: Cristopher Sanchez Breaks Phillies Franchise Record
- Boston Globe: Phillies' Cristopher Sanchez (May 27, 2026)
- SI.com: Cristopher Sanchez record
- MLB.com: Cristopher Sanchez NL All-Star Starter

stories.title: Ryan O'Hearn Just Drove In 10 Runs in One Game — a Pirates Record Since 1939
stories.angle: Ten RBIs in a single game. The Pirates hadn't seen anything like it since 1939. That's the kind of performance that gets lost in All-Star week noise — worth surfacing in the second half.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Ryan O'Hearn drove in 10 runs in a single game.

That's a Pittsburgh Pirates franchise record — breaking a mark from 1939.

He accounted for every one of those RBIs himself.

This happened just before the All-Star break. Let's talk about it.

#Pirates #MLB

article.headline: Ryan O'Hearn Drove In 10 Runs in One Game — and the Pirates Record Books Had to Be Rewritten

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Ryan O'Hearn's 10-RBI Game

#### X/Twitter Image Concept
**Style:** Franchise record badge graphic
**Visual:** Pittsburgh Pirates black-and-gold color scheme. Central badge icon with a baseball crossed by two bats. Large "10 RBI" number in gold. Below: "Pittsburgh Pirates Single-Game Record — Previous Mark: 1939."
**Text Overlay:** "@RyanOHearn" handle in smaller type, brand bug bottom right.

#### Facebook Image Concept
**Style:** Historic achievement card
**Visual:** Weathered parchment texture overlaid with Pirates colors. Old newspaper-style header: "87 YEARS. ONE GAME. 10 RBIs." Subtext: "Ryan O'Hearn breaks the Pittsburgh Pirates franchise record for RBIs in a single game." PNC Park silhouette at the bottom.
**Text Overlay:** Minimal — let the stat speak.

---

research.facts:
Ryan O'Hearn 10 RBI Game — Pirates Franchise Record
**Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Ryan O'Hearn set Pittsburgh Pirates franchise record with 10 RBIs in one game — CONFIRMED (FOX Sports milestones tracker, ESPN)
- Previous record stood since 1939 — CONFIRMED
- First player to account for each of his team's first 10+ RBIs individually in a game — CONFIRMED
- Game occurred approximately July 12-14, just before All-Star break — MEDIUM (exact date not indexed, "Tuesday" cited in research)
**Sources:**
- FOX Sports: 2026 MLB Milestones Records Tracker
- ESPN daily leaders

stories.title: The Trade Deadline Is July 31 — Who Are the Key Names?
stories.angle: Skubal is the deadline's defining move. The team that gets him likely becomes the World Series favorite in their league. Everything else is context around that one deal.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Trade deadline: July 31. 15 days.

The biggest name: Tarik Skubal. Two-time AL Cy Young. Contract year. No extension talks.

Yankees, Phillies, Rays, Cubs, Braves all in.

The team that lands Skubal becomes the World Series favorite in their league.

#MLB #TradeDeadline

article.headline: The 2026 MLB Trade Deadline Is 15 Days Away — Here's What You Need to Know

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Trade Deadline 15 Days Out

#### X/Twitter Image Concept
**Style:** Countdown clock graphic
**Visual:** Dark background with a large countdown ticker displaying "15 DAYS." Below: "MLB TRADE DEADLINE — JULY 31, 6 PM ET." Bottom row: five team logos (Yankees, Phillies, Rays, Cubs, Braves) under "SKUBAL SUITORS."
**Text Overlay:** "#TradeDeadline" hashtag bottom left.

#### Facebook Image Concept
**Style:** Trade market infographic
**Visual:** Clean white-on-dark layout. Center: Tarik Skubal name card with "2x AL CY YOUNG | CONTRACT YEAR" tag. Arrow pointing right to five team logo icons labeled "BUYERS." Arrow pointing left from Tigers logo labeled "SELLER?" Bottom strip: "JULY 31 — 15 DAYS."
**Text Overlay:** "THE DEADLINE DEAL THAT CHANGES EVERYTHING."

---

research.facts:
Trade Deadline — Key Names and Teams
**Facts:**
- Deadline: July 31, 2026, 6 PM ET — CONFIRMED (CBS Sports, Just Baseball; one outlier: Aug. 3 — likely error)
- Tarik Skubal (Tigers): 2x AL Cy Young winner, pending FA, extension talks "nil" — CONFIRMED (ESPN, CBS Sports)
- Skubal suitors: Yankees, Phillies, Rays, Cubs, Braves — CONFIRMED (ESPN, CBS Sports, TJ Stats)
- Mason Miller (Padres closer): Yankees very interested — CONFIRMED (FOX Sports)
- Padres leaning toward selling — CONFIRMED (CBS Sports)
- Astros finalizing trade: LHP Lance McCullers Jr. + Colton Gordon → Milwaukee Brewers — CONFIRMED (MLB.com, July 15)
- Mets described as "only clear seller" in NL East — CONFIRMED (CBS Sports)
- Other buyers: Yankees (catcher), Cubs (SP+RP), Phillies (multiple relievers + SP), Rays (targeting Skubal aggressively) — CONFIRMED
**Sources:**
- CBS Sports: 2026 MLB trade deadline buyers/sellers guide
- ESPN: Ranking MLB trade deadline candidates
- FOX Sports: 2026 MLB trade deadline rumors
- TJ Stats: MLB trade deadline primer 2026
- MLB.com: Trade rumors and deadline news July 15

stories.title: Ohtani Hit His 300th Career Home Run — and Schwarber Is Running Away With the MLB Lead
stories.angle: Ohtani 300. Schwarber 32. Crow-Armstrong 20/20. The first half produced extraordinary power numbers — now the second half has to live up to them.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
First half power numbers:

🟣 Shohei Ohtani: 300th career HR
🔴 Kyle Schwarber: 32 HR (MLB lead)
   — 3rd most HR in first 5 seasons with a team (Ruth, McGwire)
🐻 Pete Crow-Armstrong: 20 HR / 20+ SB before game 95
   — one of 4 players to do it in multiple seasons

#MLB

article.headline: Ohtani Hit No. 300. Schwarber Leads MLB. Crow-Armstrong Did It Again. The First Half Power Numbers Were Historic.

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
First Half Power Numbers

#### X/Twitter Image Concept
**Style:** Three-stat power card
**Visual:** Gradient background (dark to medium grey). Three horizontal stat rows, each with player headshot placeholder circle, name, and milestone: "OHTANI — 300 Career HR" / "SCHWARBER — 32 HR (MLB Lead)" / "CROW-ARMSTRONG — 20 HR / 20+ SB Before Game 95." Gold accent line separating rows.
**Text Overlay:** "THE FIRST HALF POWER NUMBERS" across top.

#### Facebook Image Concept
**Style:** First-half power retrospective banner
**Visual:** Wide-format graphic with three panels. Panel 1: Dodger blue accent, Ohtani "300" in massive type. Panel 2: Phillies red accent, Schwarber "32 HR" bold. Panel 3: Cubs blue accent, Crow-Armstrong "20/20" with a speed-and-power dual icon. Header: "FIRST HALF: THE POWER NUMBERS."
**Text Overlay:** Small historical context lines under each panel stat.

research.facts:
Ohtani 300 HR + Schwarber Leads MLB + Crow-Armstrong 20/20
**Facts (MEDIUM-HIGH to HIGH confidence):**
- Shohei Ohtani hit 300th career home run ~July 14, 2026 — CONFIRMED (ESPN daily leaders, FOX Sports milestones tracker)
- Kyle Schwarber: 32 HR, leads all of MLB — CONFIRMED (FOX Sports HR leaders, ESPN)
- Schwarber: 3rd most HR in first 5 seasons with a team in MLB history, behind Ruth (235, NYY) and McGwire (220, STL) — CONFIRMED (FOX Sports)
- Pete Crow-Armstrong (Cubs): 20 HR + 21 HRs — CONFIRMED; 4th player to reach 20 HR/20 SB before team's 95th game in multiple seasons (did it in 2025 and 2026) — MEDIUM-HIGH (cited by Breakaway Stats; ESPN confirmation recommended)
- Tyler Tolbert (team unclear): tied all-time MLB record with hits in 12 consecutive plate appearances, tying Johnny Kling (1902), Pinky Higgins (1938), Walt Dropo (1952), Jose Miranda (2024) — MEDIUM-HIGH (Baseball Reference milestone tracker)
- NOTE: William Contreras hit streak (18 games active) — MEDIUM confidence, single source
**Sources:**
- FOX Sports: 2026 MLB Milestones Records Tracker
- ESPN daily leaders
- Breakaway Stats
- Baseball Reference milestone tracker
